description: Fond of games and amusement; lighthearted and full of fun. 'Liroa' names the quality of playfulness—the spirit that finds joy in play, that approaches life with lightness.
sound_symbolism: The word dances lightly. 'Li' begins with bright ease, lightness present. 'Ro' rolls with joyful energy, play in motion. Finally 'a' opens to delight, playfulness complete.
grammatical_notes: Functions as an adjective: 'liroa lopia' (playful child). Related to 'loshi' (joyful) but emphasizes the active expression of joy through play; the verb neighbor is 'wile' (play).

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Playful mind releases grasping—the lightness of play dissolves the heaviness of attachment.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Play taught essential skills—children's games prepared them for adult work and cooperation.
